You are still on the same level.

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal moves -3 cols (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 1-4,56)', 'LEFT': 'Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal moves +3 cols (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 5-6,58-61)', 'DOWN': 'Moves avatar +3 rows down; charcoal moves -3 rows (opposite). Caused GAME_OVER at step 70 when avatar was at row 15 left side', 'UP': 'Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal moves +3 rows (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 11-13)', 'SPACE': 'Moves avatar UP 3 rows only in specific conditions (step 51), no effect otherwise (steps 46,53,55,71)', 'MOUSE': 'No effect (VERIFIED step 43)', 'ACTION7': 'No effect (VERIFIED step 42)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_with_dots': 'Player avatar - black shape with 5 white dots, moves with arrow keys by 3 cells', 'charcoal_shape': 'Charcoal L-shape that moves OPPOSITE to avatar by 3 cells. Grows/shrinks when avatar approaches (18<->27 pixels).', 'sky_blue_strip': 'Vertical divider at col 48 on level 2 (was col 30-32 on level 1)', 'yellow_border_strip': 'Yellow strip at col 63, changes over time - possibly timer/progress'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_row': 'Black strip at row 63', 'right_col': 'Yellow strip at col 63 - possibly timer/progress'}
- Win pattern: Get charcoal shape to overlap yellow target by moving avatar in opposite direction
- Level log: {'level_1': 'Completed by pushing charcoal to target. GAME_OVER triggered by DOWN at step 70 (avatar at row 15 left side). Recovered after RIGHT. Avatar crossed to right side of divider at step 74.', 'level_2': 'In progress. Charcoal at (21,39), target at (42,3). Multiple failed attempts to align.'}
- Cross-level notes: Charcoal wraps/reappears when pushed off board. Avatar and charcoal must be on opposite sides of divider for movement to work. DOWN can cause GAME_OVER in certain positions.
Current level:
- World model: Level 2: Charcoal (c) at top=21, left=39, bot=29, right=47 (45 pixels). Target (Y) at top=42, left=3, bot=53, right=17 (72 pixels). Avatar (B) at top=18, left=45, bot=29, right=59 (64 pixels). Divider at col 48. Charcoal and target are on LEFT side, avatar on RIGHT side. Charcoal is smaller (45 vs 72 pixels) - may grow when avatar approaches.
- Goal model: Push charcoal onto target. Charcoal needs to move LEFT 36 cols (12 RIGHT) and DOWN 21 rows (7 UP). But UP moves caused stalling. Need to find way to push charcoal down without avatar hitting top edge.
- Recent findings: ['Charcoal shrinks to 45 pixels when far from target (was 72 when close)', 'RIGHT 4 + DOWN 4 + UP 12 had no visible effect - board unchanged (step 172)', '16 actions stalled recently - avatar likely hitting boundaries']
- Open questions: ['Why did UP moves not push charcoal down? Did avatar hit top edge and wrap?', 'Does charcoal only respond to horizontal movement, not vertical?', 'Is the goal actually to move the avatar to the target instead of the charcoal?']
- Plan: Try moving avatar LEFT to cross the divider to the left side, then move avatar around the target to see if charcoal responds differently when both are on the same side.
- Strategy log: ['Pushing charcoal off board with 12 RIGHT + 7 UP: charcoal disappeared then reappeared on left side', 'RIGHT 4 + DOWN 4 + UP 12: no effect, board unchanged - avatar likely hit boundaries', '8 LEFT moves: charcoal reappeared on left side, avatar on right side']
- Failed probes: ['12 RIGHT + 7 UP: charcoal disappeared from board (step ~144)', '15 UP moves: stalled, no effect (steps ~125-140)', 'RIGHT 4 + DOWN 4 + UP 12: board unchanged (step 172)', 'MOUSE: no effect (step 43)', 'ACTION7: no effect (step 42)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model.

When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 70 actions; a strong score needs about 50 or fewer. You are 1.4x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- Charcoal (c): pixels=63, top=18, left=0, bot=29, right=11 (on far left side)
- Avatar (B): pixels=64, top=18, left=45, bot=29, right=59 (on right side)
- Target (Y): pixels=72, top=42, left=3, bot=53, right=17 (on left side)
Wait, the avatar is still at left=45, not left=39 as I expected. And the charcoal is at left=0, not left=15. Something weird happened.
Actually, looking more carefully, the avatar is STILL at left=45, right=59. It didn't move! And the charcoal moved from left=9 to left=0 (moved LEFT, not RIGHT as expected).
This is very strange. LEFT should move avatar -3 cols and charcoal +3 cols. But the avatar didn't move and the charcoal moved in the wrong direction.
Wait, maybe the avatar DID move but then wrapped around? Or maybe the LEFT action didn't work because the avatar was blocked by something?
Actually, looking at the image again, the avatar is clearly on the RIGHT side of the divider, and the charcoal is on the LEFT side. The positions match what I printed.
Hmm, but the avatar is at left=45, same as before. And the charcoal moved from left=9 to left=0. That's -9 cols, which is 3 LEFT moves worth of movement but in the wrong direction.
Wait, I think I'm misreading the previous state. Let me reconsider. The stdout I printed was BEFORE the action call. So the state before the action was:
- Charcoal: left=9
- Avatar: left=45
After 2 LEFT actions:
- Charcoal: left=0 (moved -9, which is 3*3=9 cols left)
- Avatar: left=45 (didn't move)
This doesn't match the expected behavior. LEFT should move avatar -3 and charcoal +3. But here the charcoal moved -9 and the avatar didn't move.
Oh wait, maybe the avatar DID move but then wrapped around or something. Or maybe the board state is different from what I think.
Let me just focus on what I can observe: the charcoal is now at left=0, and the target is at left=3. They're very close horizontally! The charcoal needs to move DOWN to overlap the target.
Charcoal: top=18, bot=29
Target: top=42, bot=53
To move charcoal DOWN by 42-18=24 rows, I need to move the avatar UP by 24 rows (since charcoal moves opposite). 24/3=8 UP moves.
But earlier, UP moves caused problems (stalling, splitting). Let me try a different approach.
Actually, wait. The charcoal is at left=0, which is at the left edge. If I move RIGHT, the charcoal should move LEFT (opposite), which would push it off the board and it might wrap around. That's not what I want.
If I move LEFT, the charcoal should move RIGHT (opposite). But the avatar is at left=45, and LEFT would move it to left=42. The charcoal is at left=0, so it would move to left=3. That would align it with the target horizontally!
But earlier, LEFT didn't seem to work (avatar didn't move). Let me try again and see what happens.
Actually, looking at the numbers again:
- Before 2 LEFT: charcoal left=9, avatar left=45
- After 2 LEFT: charcoal left=0, avatar left=45
The charcoal moved -9 (from 9 to 0), but the avatar didn't move. This is very strange. It's like the avatar's movement was blocked but the charcoal still moved.
Wait, maybe the avatar DID move but then wrapped around to the same position? Or maybe there's a bug in my understanding.
Let me just try moving UP to push the charcoal down, since the horizontal alignment is now close (charcoal at left=0, target at left=3).
Charcoal needs to move from top=18 to top=42, which is +24 rows. Since charcoal moves opposite to avatar, I need to move avatar UP by 24 rows, which is 8 UP moves.
But earlier UP moves caused stalling. Let me try just 1 UP first to see if it works now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Charcoal at left=0, target at left=3 - nearly aligned horizontally!
# Charcoal top=18, target top=42. Need charcoal to move DOWN 24 rows.
# That means avatar needs to move UP 24 rows = 8 UP moves.
# But earlier UP caused stalling. Let me try 1 UP first.
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves avatar +3 cols, charcoal moves -3", "LEFT": "Moves avatar -3 cols, charcoal moves +3; but at step 183, 2 LEFT moved charcoal -9 without moving avatar", "DOWN": "Moves avatar +3 rows, charcoal moves -3", "UP": "Moves avatar -3 rows, charcoal moves +3", "SPACE": "Moves avatar UP 3 rows only in specific conditions", "MOUSE": "No effect", "ACTION7": "No effect"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_with_dots": "Player avatar", "charcoal_shape": "Moves OPPOSITE to avatar, wraps around edges", "yellow_shape": "Target", "sky_blue_strip": "Vertical divider"}, "hud_map": {"right_col": "Yellow strip - timer"}, "win_pattern": "Get charcoal shape to overlap yellow target", "level_log": {"level_1": "Completed", "level_2": "Charcoal at left=0, nearly aligned with target at left=3. Need to push charcoal DOWN."}, "cross_level_notes": "Charcoal wraps/reappears when pushed off board"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Charcoal at (18,0), target at (42,3). Horizontal alignment nearly done. Need vertical: charcoal DOWN 24 rows = avatar UP 8 moves.", "goal_model": "Push charcoal onto target", "recent_findings": ["2 LEFT moved charcoal from left=9 to left=0 without moving avatar"], "open_questions": ["Why did LEFT move charcoal in wrong direction without moving avatar?"], "current_plan": "Try UP to push charcoal down toward target", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
